turns.user_message: Someone made a quick joke about my outfit, and it really lingered in my head all afternoon. I used to just laugh it off and move on without a second thought, but today it felt like a spotlight on everything I do wrong. I keep replaying the exact tone of their voice and wondering what I missed. It’s just a silly moment, but I can’t shake this heavy feeling that I’m somehow falling short again. I know I’m overthinking it, but the doubt just won’t quiet down.

That same offhand comment keeps echoing in my mind, and I realize it’s not just a one-time thing anymore. Lately, every little remark or sideways glance feels like proof that I’m not measuring up to basic expectations. I used to brush these things off, but now I’m constantly scanning for hidden criticism in every conversation. I catch myself apologizing for taking up space and shrinking my voice before I even speak. It’s exhausting to live inside my own head when I’m convinced I’m always getting it wrong.

I can’t stop circling back to that casual remark, and it’s completely warped how I see myself now. What used to be background noise has turned into a loud, constant reminder that I’m fundamentally inadequate. I find myself avoiding mirrors and second-guessing every single choice I make, terrified that I’m just fooling everyone around me. The more I try to push it away, the heavier it sits on my chest, convincing me that I’ll never be enough. I feel completely hollowed out by the certainty that I’m just a walking disappointment.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This used to not bother the speaker.
